callRationale: Started with a broad freshness-year search for official TypeScript or JavaScript release notes after August 2025.
Fetched the official TypeScript 6.0 announcement as an initial fresh candidate, but treated it as weaker because the source says it remains API compatible with TypeScript 5.9.
Ran a targeted search for release notes with explicit API changes and found official LangChain JavaScript v1 and Next.js 16 sources.
Fetched official LangChain, Next.js 16 release, and Next.js upgrade pages to compare candidates.

strengths: Used only the Parallel MCP provider through the benchmark harness.

Grounded the answer in official release notes and official upgrade documentation.
Identified more than three concrete API changes with source-backed code shapes.
weaknesses: Parallel's exposed schema did not provide an explicit date-range parameter, so freshness was expressed in objectives and queries and then verified from official source content.

notableFailures: Direct fix and needs no further changes: the initial broad search returned third-party and less-targeted sources, so follow-up calls narrowed to official release and upgrade pages.
